boundaryClaims.implementedScope: non-numbered cross-cutting proof target over accepted evidence

designerWorkflowSteps.designerAction: Identify the source material, mappings, and policy refs that govern the Button scenario.
designerWorkflowSteps.label: Declare design authority
designerWorkflowSteps.proofTrace: Accepted P2 and source-conformance refs establish declared source authority for the trace.

designerWorkflowSteps.designerAction: Confirm that bounded source material compiles into a governed catalog with evidence.
designerWorkflowSteps.label: Compile governed contracts
designerWorkflowSteps.proofTrace: The P2 governed catalog, ingestion report, and P2 evidence are hash-bound accepted inputs.

designerWorkflowSteps.designerAction: Check that allowed, invalid, and review-required output stays inside catalog authority.
designerWorkflowSteps.label: Generate inside the catalog boundary
designerWorkflowSteps.proofTrace: Ingestion diagnostics, source review queue output, inert P3 review queue refs, and static target projections preserve catalog boundaries instead of inferring UI.
designerWorkflowSteps.statusNote: This indexes bounded generated artifacts; it does not implement a live generator or product workflow.

designerWorkflowSteps.designerAction: Review evidence status, promotion status, reports, diagnostics, and demos as presentation only.
designerWorkflowSteps.label: Inspect evidence, not only pixels
designerWorkflowSteps.proofTrace: The trace selection plus accepted phase and target evidence make the current status inspectable before demos are considered.

designerWorkflowSteps.designerAction: Route unsupported or review-required output to source material, mapping, policy, review ownership, or future proof scope.
designerWorkflowSteps.label: Decide or revise at the authority layer
designerWorkflowSteps.proofTrace: Source review queue, P3 review queue, P4 decision ledger, review report, and evaluation report keep decisions evidence-bound.
designerWorkflowSteps.statusNote: P4 status is pass while promotionStatus is blocked; the trace preserves that governed outcome.

designerWorkflowSteps.designerAction: Identify only hash-bound static protocol or native output backed by target evidence as handoff candidates.
designerWorkflowSteps.label: Hand off only proven target output
designerWorkflowSteps.proofTrace: Protocol and native selections, projections, envelopes, packets, and reports are inert proof-only handoff artifacts.
designerWorkflowSteps.statusNote: Target artifacts remain upstream proof refs for the accepted Button path; the expired governed exception is not handoff-allowed.

designerWorkflowSteps.designerAction: Regenerate proof artifacts and evidence when source authority, policy, review decisions, or target requirements change.
designerWorkflowSteps.label: Govern changes over time
designerWorkflowSteps.proofTrace: Accepted boundary refs and evidence hashes define the current change-governance closure for this trace.
designerWorkflowSteps.statusNote: Any source, policy, review, or target change requires refreshed proof evidence before downstream trust.

diagnostics.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace fixtures cannot claim product workflow implementation, customer validation, production adoption, live integrations, live agent execution, tool calls, network calls, callbacks, secrets, action execution, work-order execution, production adapters, APIs, SDKs, runtimes, A2UI, P6, or P7.
diagnostics.suggestedAction: Keep the trace proof-only; add a target proof before claiming live or production behavior.
diagnostics.trigger: Trace fixture claims product workflow implementation, customer validation, production adoption, live behavior, live agent or work-order execution, A2UI, P6, or P7

diagnostics.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace evidence hash does not match generated artifacts.
diagnostics.suggestedAction: Regenerate designer workflow trace artifacts from accepted evidence.
diagnostics.trigger: Designer workflow trace evidence hash differs from generated artifacts

diagnostics.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace upstream evidence or artifact hash does not match accepted evidence.
diagnostics.suggestedAction: Restore accepted P2, source-conformance, P3, P4, protocol, and native evidence before regenerating the trace.
diagnostics.trigger: Required designer workflow trace evidence or artifact hash does not match the accepted boundary

diagnostics.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace upstream evidence is missing.
diagnostics.suggestedAction: Restore accepted P2, source-conformance, P3, P4, protocol, and native evidence before regenerating the trace.
diagnostics.trigger: Required designer workflow trace evidence or artifact input is missing

diagnostics.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace preserves review-required or blocked status as indexed evidence.
diagnostics.suggestedAction: Preserve review-required and blocked statuses as indexed evidence.
diagnostics.trigger: Trace fixture requires review-required and blocked status to remain visible in the index

diagnostics.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace indexes expired source-conformance review metadata as blocked evidence.
diagnostics.suggestedAction: Preserve review-required and blocked statuses as indexed evidence.
diagnostics.trigger: Trace fixture requires expired source-conformance review metadata to remain visible in the index

diagnostics.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace fixture omits a required target handoff.
diagnostics.suggestedAction: Keep both implemented static handoff targets in the trace fixture.
diagnostics.trigger: Trace fixture omits a required target handoff

diagnostics.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace fixture references a component outside the selected trace scope.
diagnostics.suggestedAction: Keep the first designer workflow trace scoped to the accepted Button catalog component.
diagnostics.trigger: Trace fixture references a component outside the selected scenario scope

diagnosticsRegistry.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace upstream evidence is missing.
diagnosticsRegistry.suggestedAction: Restore accepted P2, source-conformance, P3, P4, protocol, and native evidence before regenerating the trace.

diagnosticsRegistry.trigger: Required designer workflow trace evidence or artifact hash does not match the accepted boundary

diagnosticsRegistry.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace fixture references a component outside the selected trace scope.
diagnosticsRegistry.suggestedAction: Keep the first designer workflow trace scoped to the accepted Button catalog component.
diagnosticsRegistry.trigger: Trace fixture references a component outside the selected scenario scope

diagnosticsRegistry.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace fixture omits a required target handoff.
diagnosticsRegistry.suggestedAction: Keep both implemented static handoff targets in the trace fixture.
diagnosticsRegistry.trigger: Trace fixture omits a required target handoff

diagnosticsRegistry.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace preserves review-required or blocked status as indexed evidence.
diagnosticsRegistry.suggestedAction: Preserve review-required and blocked statuses as indexed evidence.
diagnosticsRegistry.trigger: Trace fixture requires review-required and blocked status to remain visible in the index

diagnosticsRegistry.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace indexes expired source-conformance review metadata as blocked evidence.
diagnosticsRegistry.suggestedAction: Preserve review-required and blocked statuses as indexed evidence.

diagnosticsRegistry.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace fixtures cannot claim product workflow implementation, customer validation, production adoption, live integrations, live agent execution, tool calls, network calls, callbacks, secrets, action execution, work-order execution, production adapters, APIs, SDKs, runtimes, A2UI, P6, or P7.
diagnosticsRegistry.suggestedAction: Keep the trace proof-only; add a target proof before claiming live or production behavior.
diagnosticsRegistry.trigger: Trace fixture claims product workflow implementation, customer validation, production adoption, live behavior, live agent or work-order execution, A2UI, P6, or P7

diagnosticsRegistry.canonicalMessage: Designer workflow trace evidence hash does not match generated artifacts.
diagnosticsRegistry.suggestedAction: Regenerate designer workflow trace artifacts from accepted evidence.
diagnosticsRegistry.trigger: Designer workflow trace evidence hash differs from generated artifacts

nonAuthorityStatement: This report is an index over accepted evidence. It is not catalog authority, proof authority for upstream phases, review authority, demo authority, customer validation, production adoption, live SurfaceOps, live JudgmentKit, live agent execution, tool calls, network calls, callbacks, secrets, action execution, work-order execution, a production adapter, API, SDK, runtime, A2UI, P6, or P7.
scopeStatement: First executable designer workflow trace indexes the accepted Button evidence path across P2, source-conformance, P3, P4, and the implemented static P5 targets.

exceptionLifecycle.approvedRationale: Brand exception changes action emphasis and requires source-owner review.
exceptionLifecycle.expiredRationale: Expired brand exception must block until source-owner review metadata is renewed.
